title: Acrorumohra obtusissima
taxonomy: Acrorumohra obtusissima is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is a type of flowering plant commonly known as a "voodoo lily" due to its unusual and pungent odor. The plant belongs to the subfamily Monardoideae, which is part of the family Arum. Acrorumohra obtusissima is native to tropical Asia. The exact classification of this plant is still being studied by botanists.
uses: Acrorumohra obtusissima is a plant species that is known to have several medicinal uses. It is traditionally used in some parts of the world to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
